summary: Research on the marine green alga *Bryopsis plumosa* centered on the molecular characterization of a novel lectin, BPL-4, which was isolated and identified using SDS-PAGE and MALDI-TOF mass spectrometry, revealing a molecular weight of 12.9 kDa and a single open reading frame. Comparative sequence analysis demonstrated that BPL-4 shares 60.6% sequence identity with the previously reported BPL-3 lectin from the same species and exhibits potential homology to the H lectin group, including conserved domains. These findings underscore the species-specific diversity of carbohydrate-binding proteins in *Bryopsis plumosa* and highlight the potential utility of BPL-4 as a tool for studying carbohydrate recognition and algal regeneration mechanisms.
